name: Elbmesh Flow Explainability Checks
summary: Checks that Action/Event flow explanations preserve Elbmesh boundaries and state current versus future implementation accurately.

checks: Explanation answers Resource, Policy, Event, Reaction, downstream Action, External Operation, View, Query, and journal questions when relevant.
Flow uses Action -> Event -> Reaction -> Action, not direct Action-to-Action mutation.
Execution trace facts are separated from Resource Events and adapter overlays.
Current implemented runtime/tests are distinguished from target CLI, generated docs, NATS, Restate, and Operation Journal behavior.
antiPatterns: Describing workflow as a direct Resource mutation chain.
Omitting journals from failure or recovery explanations.
Presenting future explain-flow CLI output as currently implemented.

items.text: The explanation identifies the target Resource and Action boundary.
Events and Reactions are shown as the only cross-Resource workflow mechanism.
Views and Queries are derived read-side outputs, not replay state.
Journals and trace gaps are included for audit/recovery context when outcomes fail or are incomplete.

blocks.description: Agent-facing flow explanation questions and preservation rule.
Execution trace logical entries, gaps, overlays, and visualization targets.
